compressed_prompt:
I am a software engineer with 7 years of experience, and I am looking for a new job. Can you create a resume for me and explain each section?

resume, sections
response:
# Professional Resume Template & Explanation

Here is a **complete resume** for a software engineer with 7 years of experience, followed by explanations for each section.

---

## 📄 THE RESUME

---

**JOHN DOE**
📧 johndoe@email.com | 📞 (555) 123-4567 | 🔗 linkedin.com/in/johndoe | 💻 github.com/johndoe
📍 San Francisco, CA

---

### PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
Results-driven Software Engineer with 7 years of experience designing, developing, and deploying scalable web applications. Proven track record of delivering high-quality solutions in fast-paced environments. Skilled in full-stack development with expertise in cloud technologies and agile methodologies.

---

### TECHNICAL SKILLS
| Category | Skills |
|---|---|
| **Languages** | Python, JavaScript, Java, TypeScript |
| **Frameworks** | React, Node.js, Django, Spring Boot |
| **Databases** | PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis |
| **Cloud/DevOps** | AWS, Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD |
| **Tools** | Git, JIRA, Jenkins, Terraform |

---

### PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

**Senior Software Engineer**
*ABC Tech Company | San Francisco, CA | Jan 2021 – Present*
- Led development of a microservices architecture that **reduced system latency by 40%**
- Mentored a team of 4 junior developers, conducting code reviews and technical training
- Designed and implemented RESTful APIs serving **2 million+ daily active users**
- Collaborated with product managers to define technical requirements and roadmaps

**Software Engineer**
*XYZ Solutions | Austin, TX | June 2018 – Dec 2020*
- Built and maintained full-stack web applications using React and Node.js
- Optimized database queries resulting in a **30% improvement in application performance**
- Implemented automated testing pipelines, increasing code coverage from 45% to 85%
- Participated in agile sprints and contributed to technical documentation

**Junior Software Engineer**
*StartUp Inc. | Remote | Aug 2016 – May 2018*
- Developed front-end features using JavaScript and React
- Fixed bugs and maintained legacy codebase in Java
- Assisted in migrating on-premise infrastructure to AWS cloud

---

### EDUCATION

**Bachelor of Science in Computer Science**
*University of California, Berkeley | Graduated: May 2016*
- GPA: 3.7/4.0
- Relevant Coursework: Data Structures, Algorithms, Software Engineering, Database Systems

---

### CERTIFICATIONS
- AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Associate *(2022)*
- Google Professional Cloud Developer *(2021)*

---

### PROJECTS
**E-Commerce Platform** | *github.com/johndoe/ecommerce*
- Built a full-stack e-commerce app using React, Node.js, and PostgreSQL
- Implemented payment processing with Stripe API handling $50K+ monthly transactions

**Open Source Contribution** | *github.com/johndoe/oss-project*
- Contributed 15+ pull requests to a popular open-source JavaScript library

---

## 📚 SECTION EXPLANATIONS

---

### 1️⃣ CONTACT INFORMATION
**What it is:** Your basic identifying information at the top of the resume.

**What to include:**
- Full name *(make it large and bold)*
- Professional email address
- Phone number
- LinkedIn profile
- GitHub profile *(critical for software engineers)*
- City and State *(full address is no longer necessary)*

> ✅ **Tip:** Use a professional email. Avoid nicknames or old emails like *coolguy123@hotmail.com*

---

### 2️⃣ PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
**What it is:** A 2-4 sentence snapshot of who you are professionally.

**Why it matters:**
- Recruiters spend only **6-7 seconds** scanning a resume initially
- This section immediately tells them your value
- It replaces the old-fashioned "Objective Statement"

**Formula:**
> *[Job Title] + [Years of Experience] + [Key Strengths] + [Value You Bring]*

> ✅ **Tip:** Customize this section for **every job application** to match the job description

---

### 3️⃣ TECHNICAL SKILLS
**What it is:** A categorized list of your technical competencies.

**Why it matters:**
- Many companies use **ATS (Applicant Tracking Systems)** that scan for keywords
- Hiring managers want to quickly see if you match their tech stack
- Keeps your experience section clean and readable

**Best practices:**
- ✅ Group skills by category
- ✅ List skills you are **actually proficient in**
- ❌ Do not include skill levels like "beginner" or "expert" bars
- ❌ Do not list every tool you have ever touched

---

### 4️⃣ PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE
**What it is:** Your work history listed in **reverse chronological order** (most recent first).

**This is the MOST IMPORTANT section.** Here is how to write it well:

**For each job include:**
| Element | Example |
|---|---|
| **Job Title** | Senior Software Engineer |
| **Company Name** | ABC Tech Company |
| **Location** | San Francisco, CA |
| **Dates** | Jan 2021 – Present |
| **Bullet Points** | Achievements and responsibilities |

**The STAR Method for bullet points:**
- **S**ituation – What was the context?
- **T**ask – What were you responsible for?
- **A**ction – What did you do?
- **R**esult – What was the outcome?

**Strong vs. Weak bullet points:**
| ❌ Weak | ✅ Strong |
|---|---|
| "Worked on APIs" | "Designed RESTful APIs serving 2M+ daily users" |
| "Helped improve performance" | "Optimized queries improving performance by 30%" |
| "Was part of a team" | "Led a team of 4 engineers to deliver project on time" |

> ✅ **Tip:** Always **quantify your achievements** with numbers, percentages, or dollar amounts

---

### 5️⃣ EDUCATION
**What it is:** Your academic background and degrees.

**What to include:**
- Degree name and major
- University name and location
- Graduation year
- GPA *(only if 3.5 or above)*
- Relevant coursework *(optional, more useful for recent graduates)*

> ✅ **Tip:** With 7 years of experience, keep this section **brief**. Your experience matters more than your degree at this stage.

---

### 6️⃣ CERTIFICATIONS
**What it is:** Industry-recognized credentials that validate your skills.

**Why it matters for software engineers:**
- Shows commitment to continuous learning
- Cloud certifications *(AWS, GCP, Azure)* are **highly valued**
- Can compensate if your degree is not in Computer Science

**Popular certifications to consider:**
- AWS Certified Solutions Architect
- Google Professional Cloud Developer
- Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA)
- Microsoft Azure Developer Associate

---

### 7️⃣ PROJECTS
**What it is:** Personal, freelance, or open-source projects you have built.

**Why it matters:**
- Demonstrates passion for coding beyond work hours
- Shows practical skills with real examples
- Especially important if switching industries or tech stacks

**What to include:**
- Project name and link to GitHub
- Technologies used
- Brief description of what it does
- Impact or scale if applicable

> ✅ **Tip:** Keep your GitHub profile **active and clean** as employers will likely visit it

---

## 🎯 GENERAL RESUME TIPS

| Tip | Details |
|---|---|
| **Length** | 1-2 pages maximum for 7 years of experience |
| **Format** | Clean, simple, ATS-friendly format |
| **Font** | Use professional fonts like Calibri, Arial, or Garamond |
| **Customize** | Tailor your resume for each job application |
| **Proofread** | Zero spelling or grammar errors |
| **File Type** | Save and send as **PDF** unless told otherwise |
| **ATS Friendly** | Avoid tables, graphics, and unusual formatting |

---

## 🔑 KEY TAKEAWAY

> Your resume is a **marketing document**, not a biography.
